conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children under the age of 16 (or under 20 if they're in approved education or training)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex who is 7, Blake who is 12, and Charlie who is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know you have three children - Alex (7), Blake (12), and Charlie (10).

Now, do all three children currently live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, they do live with me, but I should mention that I don't actually live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is an important detail.

Can you tell me which country you're currently living in, and also whether you or your partner (if you have one) work for the UK Civil Service, the armed forces, or for an organisation funded by the UK government?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in France at the moment. And no, neither I nor my partner work for the UK Civil Service, the armed forces, or any UK government-funded organisation. We have regular jobs here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. 

Just to confirm - are you or your partner receiving any UK State Pension or other UK benefit payments while living in France?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we're not receiving any UK State Pension or other UK benefit payments.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I have a few more questions to fully assess your situation.

Are you or your partner considered to be "subject to UK National Insurance contributions" - for example, because you're posted abroad temporarily by a UK employer who is still paying UK National Insurance for you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we're not. We're working for local employers here in France, so we're not paying UK National Insurance contributions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have one more important question:

Are you or your partner nationals of an EU/EEA country or Switzerland, or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and we're not EU/EEA nationals or Swiss nationals.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

Based on the information you've provided, I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for any of your three children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie.

**Here's why:**

While your children meet the age requirements (all being under 16) and live with you, the issue is that you currently live in France. To claim Child Benefit while living outside the UK, you would need to meet at least one of these specific conditions:

- Work for the UK Civil Service, armed forces, or a UK government-funded organisation
- Receive UK State Pension or certain other UK benefits
- Be subject to UK National Insurance contributions
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or be an EU/EEA or Swiss national

Since you and your partner work for local employers in France and don't meet any of these exceptions, you are unfortunately not eligible to claim Child Benefit while living abroad.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you may become eligible at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 7 years old and lives with the claimant, meeting the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant lives in France and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. The claimant does not work for UK Civil Service, armed forces, or UK government-funded organisations; is not receiving UK State Pension or other UK benefits; is not subject to UK National Insurance contributions; and does not have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme nor is an EU/EEA/Swiss national. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 12 years old and lives with the claimant, meeting the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant lives in France and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. The claimant does not work for UK Civil Service, armed forces, or UK government-funded organisations; is not receiving UK State Pension or other UK benefits; is not subject to UK National Insurance contributions; and does not have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme nor is an EU/EEA/Swiss national. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 10 years old and lives with the claimant, meeting the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant lives in France and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. The claimant does not work for UK Civil Service, armed forces, or UK government-funded organisations; is not receiving UK State Pension or other UK benefits; is not subject to UK National Insurance contributions; and does not have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme nor is an EU/EEA/Swiss national. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) who all meet the age requirements and live with them. However, the claimant currently lives in France and does not meet any of the specific exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. To be eligible while living outside the UK, claimants must work for UK Civil Service/armed forces/UK government-funded organisations, receive UK State Pension or other UK benefits, be subject to UK National Insurance contributions, or have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme or be EU/EEA/Swiss nationals. Since none of these apply, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for any of their children.
